707	  % $$
708	  % \item if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
709	  % $H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
710	  % \end{enumerate}"
711	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH (``Flat base change''), the
712	  \(i = 0\) case.}
713	  Consider the cartesian square
714	  \[
715	    \begin{array}{ccc}
716	      X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
717	      {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
718	      S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
719	    \end{array}
720	  \]
721	  with \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and
722	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\). Assume that \(g\) is flat and that \(f\)
723	  is quasi-compact and quasi-separated. Then the base-change map of
724	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an isomorphism
725	  \[
726	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
727	    f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr) = f'_*\mathcal{F}' .
728	  \]
729	  Equivalently, in the affine situation \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\),
730	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat, the comparison map
731	  \[
732	    H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
733	    H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
734	  \]
735	  is an isomorphism, where \(X_B = X \times_{\operatorname{Spec}(A)}
736	  \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) and \(\mathcal{F}_B\) is the pullback of
737	  \(\mathcal{F}\).
738	
739	  The hypothesis that \(\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent is essential: it is what
740	  makes \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent on the affine base and thus the tilde of
741	  a module, and it is what lets the affine reduction
742	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}) write \(\mathcal{F}\) as
743	  \(\widetilde{M}\) over each affine open. Without it the comparison map need not be
744	  an isomorphism.
745	\end{theorem}
746	
747	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Having said this, part (1) follows from part (2). Namely,
748	% part (1) is local on $S'$ and hence we may assume $S$
749	% and $S'$ are affine. In other words, we have $S = \Spec(A)$
750	% and $S' = \Spec(B)$ as in (2).
751	% Then since $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is quasi-coherent
752	% (Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images}),
753	% it is the quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_S$-module associated to the
754	% $A$-module $H^0(S, R^if_*\mathcal{F}) = H^i(X, \mathcal{F})$
755	% (equality by
756	% Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}).
757	% Similarly, $R^if'_*\mathcal{F}'$ is the quasi-coherent
758	% $\mathcal{O}_{S'}$-module associated to the $B$-module
759	% $H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$. Since pullback by $g$ corresponds
760	% to $- \otimes_A B$ on modules
761	% (Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-widetilde-pullback})
762	% we see that it suffices to prove (2).
763	%
764	% Let $A \to B$ be a flat ring homomorphism.
765	% Let $X$ be a quasi-compact and quasi-separated scheme over $A$.
766	% Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
767	% Set $X_B = X \times_{\Spec(A)} \Spec(B)$ and denote
768	% $\mathcal{F}_B$ the pullback of $\mathcal{F}$.
769	% We are trying to show that the map
770	% $$
771	% H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \longrightarrow H^i(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
772	% $$
773	% (given by the reference in the statement of the lemma)
774	% is an isomorphism.
775	%
776	% In case $X$ is separated, choose an affine open covering
777	% $\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \ldots \cup U_t$ and recall that
778	% $$
779	% \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F}),
780	% $$
781	% see
782	% Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}.
783	% If $\mathcal{U}_B : X_B = (U_1)_B \cup \ldots \cup (U_t)_B$ we obtain
784	% by base change, then it is still the case that each $(U_i)_B$ is affine
785	% and that $X_B$ is separated. Thus we obtain
786	% $$
787	% \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) = H^p(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B).
788	% $$
789	% We have the following relation between the {\v C}ech complexes
790	% $$
791	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) =
792	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B
793	% $$
794	% as follows from
795	% Lemma \ref{lemma-affine-base-change}.
796	% Since $A \to B$ is flat, the same thing remains true on taking cohomology."
797	% NOTE: Lean proof is a documented sorry (deferred). Its affine input is the
798	% affine lemma above, which in turn reduces to lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso (the
799	% affine-pushforward-of-tilde brick) plus the tilde full-faithfulness reframe. On
800	% top of that it needs Čech-cohomology / affine-cover infrastructure for
801	% SheafOfModules, which Mathlib does not provide. This remains a multi-lane engine
802	% build, not a single-iter target. See informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md.
803	\begin{proof}
804	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}
805	  \emph{Reduction to the affine target.} The statement is local on \(S'\), so we
806	  may assume \(S\) and \(S'\) are affine, say \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and
807	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat. Because \(f\) is
808	  quasi-compact and quasi-separated, \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent on
809	  \(S\); it is therefore the sheaf associated to the \(A\)-module
810	  \(H^0(S, f_*\mathcal{F}) = H^0(X, \mathcal{F})\). Likewise
811	  \(f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) is the sheaf on \(S'\) associated to the \(B\)-module
812	  \(H^0(X', \mathcal{F}')\). Since pullback along \(g\) corresponds to
813	  \(- \otimes_A B\) on modules, the base-change map becomes the comparison map of
814	  \(B\)-modules
815	  \[
816	    H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \longrightarrow H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B),
817	    \qquad X_B = X \times_{\operatorname{Spec}(A)} \operatorname{Spec}(B),
818	  \]
819	  and it suffices to prove that this map is an isomorphism.
820	
821	  \medskip
822	  \emph{The separated case via {\v C}ech complexes.} Suppose first that \(X\) is
823	  separated over \(A\). Choose a finite affine open cover
824	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\); by separatedness all finite
825	  intersections \(U_{i_0 \dots i_p}\) are again affine, so {\v C}ech cohomology
826	  computes sheaf cohomology:
827	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F})\). Base changing
828	  the cover yields an affine open cover
829	  \(\mathcal{U}_B : X_B = (U_1)_B \cup \dots \cup (U_t)_B\) of the again-separated
830	  \(X_B\), with the same property
831	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) = H^p(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\). The
832	  affine case (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}) identifies each
833	  term of the base-changed {\v C}ech complex with the original term tensored over
834	  \(A\) with \(B\); that is, there is an isomorphism of cochain complexes
835	  \[
836	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) \;\cong\;
837	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B .
838	  \]
839	  Because \(A \to B\) is flat, \(- \otimes_A B\) is an exact functor and hence
840	  commutes with the formation of cohomology of a complex; taking \(H^0\)
841	  (degree-zero cohomology) gives the desired isomorphism
842	  \(H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \cong H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\).
843	
844	  \medskip
845	  \emph{The quasi-separated case.} In general \(X\) need only be quasi-separated.
846	  Choose a finite affine open cover \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\).
847	  Now the intersections \(U_{i_0 \dots i_p}\) are quasi-compact and separated
848	  rather than affine, but they fall under the separated case already treated, so
849	  flat base change holds for each of them. One then compares the two
850	  {\v C}ech-to-cohomology spectral sequences with \(E_2\)-pages
851	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F}))\) and
852	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F}_B))\), converging to
853	  \(H^{p+q}(X, \mathcal{F})\) and \(H^{p+q}(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\) respectively.
854	  The separated case provides the term-by-term identification
855	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F}_B)) =
856	  \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F})) \otimes_A B\) on the
857	  \(E_2\)-pages, and flatness of \(A \to B\) propagates this isomorphism through
858	  the spectral sequences to the abutment. In particular, in degree \(0\) one
859	  obtains \(H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \cong H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\), as
860	  required. (For the \(i = 0\) statement at hand, one may avoid the spectral
861	  sequence entirely and argue by Mayer--Vietoris induction on the number \(t\) of
862	  cover members, reducing each step to the separated case.)
863	\end{proof}

KEY FACT: by definition of pushforward, Γ(N,U) = Γ(M^~, (Spec.map φ)⁻¹ U), and (Spec.map φ)⁻¹ D(a) = D(φ a). So the restriction map is, on underlying functions, the tilde restriction Γ(M^~,⊤)=M → Γ(M^~, D(φa)) = M[1/φa], which is IsLocalizedModule at powers (φ a) over R'. I have a lemma IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars : given f : M →ₗ[R'] N with IsLocalizedModule (algebraMapSubmonoid R' (powers a)) f, then IsLocalizedModule (powers a) (f.restrictScalars R). {"symbol":"/-- **Restriction of scalars of a localized module is a localized module.** If `f : M →ₗ[A] N`
exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` at the image submonoid `Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S`
(for `S : Submonoid R` and `A` an `R`-algebra), then the `R`-linear map underlying `f`
exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` at `S` itself. This is the exact converse of
`IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars` and is the ring-change ingredient powering the affine
pushforward identification: localizing `restrictScalars φ M` at `a ∈ R` agrees with localizing
`M` at `φ a ∈ R'`. Project-local: Mathlib ships only the forward direction. -/
lemma IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars
    {R A : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A]
    {M N : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N]
    [Module R M] [Module R N] [Module A M] [Module A N]
    [IsScalarTower R A M] [IsScalarTower R A N]
    (S : Submonoid R) (f : M →ₗ[A] N)
    [IsLocalizedModule (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f] :
    IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R) where
  map_units x := by
    have h := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f
      (⟨algebraMap R A x, Submonoid.mem_map.mpr ⟨x, x.2, rfl⟩⟩ : Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S)
    simp only [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, Module.End.isUnit_iff] at h ⊢
    exact h
  surj y := by
    obtain ⟨⟨x, ⟨_, t, ht, rfl⟩⟩, e⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f y
    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using e⟩
  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} e := by
    obtain ⟨⟨_, c, hc, rfl⟩, h⟩ :=
      IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) (f := f) e
    exact ⟨⟨c, hc⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using h⟩","info":"`lemma` means the same as `theorem`. It is used to denote "less important" theorems","diagnostics":[]}

namespace IsLocalizedModule

variable {M₀ M₀'} [AddCommMonoid M₀] [AddCommMonoid M₀'] [Module R M₀] [Module R M₀']
variable (f₀ : M₀ →ₗ[R] M₀') [IsLocalizedModule S f₀]
variable {M₁ M₁'} [AddCommMonoid M₁] [AddCommMonoid M₁'] [Module R M₁] [Module R M₁']
variable (f₁ : M₁ →ₗ[R] M₁') [IsLocalizedModule S f₁]
variable {M₂ M₂'} [AddCommMonoid M₂] [AddCommMonoid M₂'] [Module R M₂] [Module R M₂']
variable (f₂ : M₂ →ₗ[R] M₂') [IsLocalizedModule S f₂]

/-- Localization of composition is the composition of localization -/
theorem map_comp' (g : M₀ →ₗ[R] M₁) (h : M₁ →ₗ[R] M₂) :
    map S f₀ f₂ (h ∘ₗ g) = map S f₁ f₂ h ∘ₗ map S f₀ f₁ g := by
  ext x
  obtain ⟨⟨x, s⟩, rfl⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.mk'_surjective S f₀ x
  simp

section Algebra

theorem mkOfAlgebra {R S S' : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [Ring S] [Ring S'] [Algebra R S]
    [Algebra R S'] (M : Submonoid R) (f : S →ₐ[R] S') (h₁ : ∀ x ∈ M, IsUnit (algebraMap R S' x))
    (h₂ : ∀ y, ∃ x : S × M, x.2 • y = f x.1) (h₃ : ∀ x, f x = 0 → ∃ m : M, m • x = 0) :
    IsLocalizedModule M f.toLinearMap := by
  replace h₃ := fun x =>
    Iff.intro (h₃ x) fun ⟨⟨m, hm⟩, e⟩ =>
      (h₁ m hm).mul_left_cancel <| by
        rw [← Algebra.smul_def]
        simpa [Submonoid.smul_def] using f.congr_arg e
  constructor
  · intro x
    rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]
    constructor
    · rintro a b (e : x • a = x • b)
      simp_rw [Submonoid.smul_def, Algebra.smul_def] at e
      exact (h₁ x x.2).mul_left_cancel e
    · intro a
      refine ⟨((h₁ x x.2).unit⁻¹ :) * a, ?_⟩
      rw [Module.algebraMap_end_apply, Algebra.smul_def, ← mul_assoc, IsUnit.mul_val_inv, one_mul]
  · exact h₂
  · intro x y
    dsimp only [AlgHom.toLinearMap_apply]
    rw [← sub_eq_zero, ← map_sub, h₃]
    simp_rw [smul_sub, sub_eq_zero]
    exact id

end Algebra

variable {R A M M' : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A] (S : Submonoid R)
  [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] [AddCommMonoid M'] [Module R M']
  [IsLocalization S A]

attribute [local instance] LocalizedModule.moduleOfIsLocalization in
/-- If `M'` is the localization of `M` at `S` and `A = S⁻¹R`, then `M'` is an `A`-module. -/
@[reducible] noncomputable def module (f : M →ₗ[R] M') [IsLocalizedModule S f] : Module A M' :=
  (IsLocalizedModule.iso S f).symm.toAddEquiv.module A

attribute [local instance] LocalizedModule.moduleOfIsLocalization in
lemma isScalarTower_module (f : M →ₗ[R] M') [IsLocalizedModule S f] :
    letI : Module A M' := IsLocalizedModule.module S f
    IsScalarTower R A M' :=
  (IsLocalizedModule.iso S f).symm.isScalarTower A

section Subsingleton

lemma mem_ker_iff (S : Submonoid R) {g : M →ₗ[R] M'}
    [IsLocalizedModule S g] {m : M} :
    m ∈ LinearMap.ker g ↔ ∃ r ∈ S, r • m = 0 := by
  simpa using IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S g

lemma subsingleton_iff_ker_eq_top (S : Submonoid R) (g : M →ₗ[R] M')
    [IsLocalizedModule S g] :
    Subsingleton M' ↔ LinearMap.ker g = ⊤ := by

      tilde ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) := by
  apply pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule φ M
  intro a
  sorry

/-- **Sections of an affine pushforward over an arbitrary open = restriction of scalars.**
The `D(a)`-level (indeed arbitrary-open) generalization of `gammaPushforwardIso`. For a ring map
`φ : R ⟶ R'`, an `Spec R'`-module `N`, and an open `U` of `Spec R` with preimage
`V = (Spec φ)⁻¹ U` in `Spec R'`, the `R`-module of sections of the pushforward
`(Spec φ)_* N` over `U` is the restriction of scalars along `φ` of the `R'`-module of sections of
`N` over `V`. Because `modulesSpecToSheaf` forgets to the *global* section ring uniformly (it
restricts scalars along the global-sections map at the top open, not at `U`), the construction is
*identical* to that of `gammaPushforwardIso` — the same restriction-of-scalars composition identity
twice plus the same `⊤`-level ring equation `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop` — with only
the evaluation open changed from `⊤` to `U` / `V`. Project-local. -/
noncomputable def gammaPushforwardIsoAt {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R')
    (N : (Spec R').Modules) (U : (Spec R).Opens) :
    (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj N)).val.obj
        (Opposite.op U) ≅
      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj
        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.obj
          (Opposite.op ((Opens.map (Spec.map φ).base).obj U))) := by
  set SecN : ModuleCat ↑((Spec R').ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
    (((SheafOfModules.forgetToSheafModuleCat (Spec R').ringCatSheaf (Opposite.op ⊤)
        (Limits.initialOpOfTerminal Limits.isTerminalTop)).obj N).val.obj
          (Opposite.op ((Opens.map (Spec.map φ).base).obj U))) with hSecN
  set gsRhom := (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R).hom.hom with hgsR
  set gsR'hom := (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R').hom.hom with hgsR'
  set pushTop := ((Spec.map φ).toRingCatSheafHom.hom.app (Opposite.op ⊤)).hom with hpush
  have hcomp : pushTop.comp gsRhom = gsR'hom.comp φ.hom := by
    apply RingHom.ext
    intro x
    simpa [hpush, hgsR, hgsR', RingHom.comp_apply] using
      congr($(globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop φ).hom x)
  exact (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App gsRhom pushTop (pushTop.comp gsRhom) rfl SecN).symm ≪≫
    eqToIso (congrArg (fun h => (ModuleCat.restrictScalars h).obj SecN) hcomp) ≪≫
    (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App φ.hom gsR'hom (gsR'hom.comp φ.hom) rfl SecN)

/-- **Affine pushforward of a tilde-module (unconditional).** For a ring map `φ : R ⟶ R'`
and an `R'`-module `M`, pushing the quasi-coherent sheaf `M^~` forward along `Spec φ` is,
input.new_string:
/-- **The structure-sheaf restriction of a tilde-module from `⊤` to `D(b)` is a localization.**
Read in `ModuleCat R'` via `modulesSpecToSheaf`, the restriction map
`Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(b))` exhibits its target as the localization of its source at
`Submonoid.powers b`. This packages the Mathlib instance
`tilde.toOpen … (basicOpen b)` (which makes `M → Γ(M^~, D(b))` a localization) together with the
triangle identity `tilde.toOpen_res` and the fact that `M → Γ(M^~, ⊤)` is bijective (localization
at the trivial submonoid `powers 1`). Project-local. -/
lemma tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule {R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') (b : R') :
    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b)
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom := by
  -- `e₀ : M → Γ(M^~, ⊤)` is bijective: it is a localization at `powers (1 : R') = {1}`.
  haveI inst1 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (1 : R'))
      (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (1 : R'))).hom := inferInstance
  rw [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one] at inst1
  have hbij : Function.Bijective (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by
    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
    · intro x y hxy
      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq
        (S := Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (f := (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom) hxy
      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
      have : (1 : R') • x = (1 : R') • y := by simpa [← hn, one_pow] using hc
      simpa using this
    · intro y
      obtain ⟨⟨x, c⟩, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj
        (Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom y
      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
      refine ⟨x, ?_⟩
      have : (1 : R') • y = (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom x := by simpa [← hn, one_pow] using hc
      simpa using this.symm
  -- The triangle identity `toOpen ⊤ ≫ ρσ = toOpen (D b)`.
  have htri := tilde.toOpen_res M (⊤ : (Spec R').Opens) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)
    (homOfLE (le_top)).op
  -- Transport the `powers b`-localization `toOpen (D b)` across the bijection `toOpen ⊤`.
  set e₀ := LinearEquiv.ofBijective (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom hbij with he₀
  set ρσ := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
    (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom with hρσ
  -- `ρσ ∘ₗ e₀ = (toOpen (D b)).hom`, which is a localization at `powers b`.
  have hcomp : ρσ ∘ₗ (e₀ : M →ₗ[R'] _) = (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom := by
    apply LinearMap.ext; intro x
    have := congr($htri x)
    simpa [hρσ, he₀, ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply] using this
  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b) (ρσ ∘ₗ (e₀ : M →ₗ[R'] _)) := by
    rw [hcomp]; infer_instance
  -- `ρσ = (ρσ ∘ₗ e₀) ∘ₗ e₀.symm`, an `of_linearEquiv_right` of the above.
  have hρσeq : ρσ = (ρσ ∘ₗ (e₀ : M →ₗ[R'] _)) ∘ₗ (e₀.symm : _ →ₗ[R'] M) := by
    ext x; simp
  rw [hρσeq]
  infer_instance

lemma tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule {R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') (b : R') :
    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b)
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom := by
  -- `toOpen M ⊤` is a localization at `powers (1 : R')` (since `basicOpen 1 = ⊤`), hence bijective.
  haveI inst1 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by
    have h1 : PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (1 : R') = ⊤ := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one
    rw [← h1]; infer_instance
  have hbij : Function.Bijective (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by
    refine ⟨fun x y hxy => ?_, fun y => ?_⟩
    · obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq
        (S := Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (f := (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom) hxy
      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
      have : (1 : R') • x = (1 : R') • y := by simpa [← hn, one_pow] using hc
      simpa using this
    · obtain ⟨⟨x, c⟩, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj
        (Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom y
      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
      refine ⟨x, ?_⟩
      have : (1 : R') • y = (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom x := by simpa [← hn, one_pow] using hc
      simpa using this.symm
  -- Make `toOpen M ⊤` an isomorphism of `ModuleCat`s.
  haveI hiso : IsIso (tilde.toOpen M ⊤) := by
    rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]; exact hbij
  -- Triangle identity: `toOpen ⊤ ≫ (restriction) = toOpen (D b)`.
  have htri := tilde.toOpen_res M (⊤ : (Spec R').Opens) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)
    (homOfLE (le_top))
  -- Hence `(restriction) = (toOpen ⊤)⁻¹ ≫ toOpen (D b)`.
  have hρσ : (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op
      = inv (tilde.toOpen M ⊤) ≫ tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b) := by
    rw [← htri, IsIso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
  rw [hρσ, ModuleCat.hom_comp]
  -- `toOpen (D b)` is the localization at `powers b`; precomposing with the equivalence
  -- `(toOpen ⊤)⁻¹` preserves the localization property.
  have he : ((inv (tilde.toOpen M ⊤)).hom) =
      (ModuleCat.homMkLinearEquiv.symm (asIso (tilde.toOpen M ⊤)).symm.toLinearEquiv) := rfl
  exact IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right
    (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom
    (asIso (tilde.toOpen M ⊤)).symm.toLinearEquiv

haveI inst1 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by
    have h := (inferInstance : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (1 : R'))
      (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (1 : R'))).hom)
    simpa only [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one] using h
  have hbij : Function.Bijective (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by
    refine ⟨fun x y hxy => ?_, fun y => ?_⟩
    · obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq
        (S := Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (f := (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom) hxy
      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
      have hc1 : (↑c : R') = 1 := by rw [← hn, one_pow]
      rw [Submonoid.smul_def, Submonoid.smul_def, hc1, one_smul, one_smul] at hc
      exact hc
    · obtain ⟨⟨x, c⟩, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj
        (Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom y
      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
      have hc1 : (↑c : R') = 1 := by rw [← hn, one_pow]
      refine ⟨x, ?_⟩
      rw [Submonoid.smul_def, hc1, one_smul] at hc
      exact hc.symm
  -- Triangle identity: `toOpen ⊤ ≫ (restriction) = toOpen (D b)`.
  have htri := tilde.toOpen_res M (⊤ : (Spec R').Opens) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)
    (homOfLE (le_top))
  -- The inverse equivalence `Γ(M^~, ⊤) ≃ M`.
  set le : M ≃ₗ[R'] ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
    LinearEquiv.ofBijective (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom hbij with hle
  -- The restriction map equals `toOpen (D b) ∘ le⁻¹`, a localization at `powers b`
  -- precomposed with a linear equivalence.
  have key : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom
      = (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom ∘ₗ le.symm.toLinearMap := by
    apply LinearMap.ext
    intro s
    obtain ⟨m, rfl⟩ := hbij.surjective s
    have htm := congr($htri m)
    simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply] at htm ⊢
    rw [show le.symm ((tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom m) = m from le.symm_apply_apply m]
    exact htm
  rw [key]

input.new_string:
apply pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule φ M
  intro a
  -- STRATEGY (`of_linearEquiv`): the restriction map `ρ : Γ(N,⊤) → Γ(N,D(a))` of
  -- `N := (Spec φ)_* (M^~)` is, under the isomorphisms
  -- `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) ⊤` and `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) (D a)`,
  -- identified with the restriction of scalars along `φ` of the `R'`-side tilde restriction
  -- `σ : Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(φ a))`. That `σ` is a localization at `powers (φ a)`
  -- (`tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule`), so its restriction of scalars along `φ` is a
  -- localization at `powers a` (`IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars`); transporting along
  -- the two isomorphisms then yields `hloc(a)`.
  letI : Algebra R R' := φ.hom.toAlgebra
  -- The bottom map `restrictScalars φ σ` is a localization at `powers a`.  This consumes the two
  -- bricks `tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule` and `IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars`.
  have hbottom : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
      (LinearMap.restrictScalars R
        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
          (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (φ.hom a) ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom) := by
    have hsubmon : Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid R' (Submonoid.powers a)
        = Submonoid.powers (φ.hom a) := by
      rw [Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid, Submonoid.map_powers]; rfl
    haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid R' (Submonoid.powers a))
        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
          (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (φ.hom a) ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom := by
      rw [hsubmon]; exact tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule M (φ.hom a)
    exact IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars (Submonoid.powers a) _
  -- REMAINING GAP: transport `hbottom` to the pushforward-side restriction map `ρ` via the
  -- naturality of `gammaPushforwardIsoAt` in the open argument (the square
  -- `e₂.hom ∘ ρ = (restrictScalars φ) σ ∘ e₁.hom` for `e₁ = gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) ⊤`,
  -- `e₂ = gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) (D a)`), composed with the preimage identification
  -- `SpecMap_preimage_basicOpen φ a : (Spec φ)⁻¹ D(a) = D(φ a)`.  Once that square is available,
  -- `ρ = e₂.symm ∘ₗ (restrictScalars φ σ) ∘ₗ e₁` and `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv` /
  -- `of_linearEquiv_right` close the goal from `hbottom`.  See task_results for the precise
  -- decomposition.
  sorry

input.content:
# Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean — iter-239

## Summary

- **Sorry count: 2 → 3.** Net +1, but the new sorry lives in a *new blueprint-pinned declaration*
  (`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`, which did **not** exist before — the `\lean{}` pin was dangling),
  and it is supported by **two new axiom-clean reusable bricks**.
- **Closed sorries:** none of the pre-existing two (`affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`,
  `flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` — both untouched, still `sorry`).
- **New axiom-clean declarations (both verified `propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound` only):**
  1. `gammaPushforwardIsoAt` — the **open-indexed generalization** of `gammaPushforwardIso`:
     `Γ((Spec φ)_* N, U) ≅ restrictScalars φ (Γ(N, (Spec φ)⁻¹ U))` for *any* open `U`. This is
     exactly blueprint movement (1) `e_{D(a)}`. Proof = literally `gammaPushforwardIso`'s body with
     `op ⊤ → op U / op V`; it typechecks because `modulesSpecToSheaf` restricts scalars to the base
     ring *uniformly at the top open* (not per-`U`), so the same `restrictScalarsComp'App`×2 +
     `eqToIso(globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop)` reconciliation works verbatim at any `U`.
  2. `tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule` — the `R'`-side fact: the tilde restriction
     `Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(b))` (read in `ModuleCat R'`) is `IsLocalizedModule (powers b)`. Proof:
     `tilde.toOpen M ⊤` is a localization at `powers 1` hence bijective; the triangle
     `tilde.toOpen_res` + `LinearEquiv.eq_comp_toLinearMap_symm` + `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`
     transport the known `powers b`-localization `tilde.toOpen M (basicOpen b)` onto the restriction.
- **New (sorry-bearing) declaration:** `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` — the unconditional brick. Its
  body reduces (via `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule`) to discharging `hloc(a)`, with
  a typed `sorry` at that point and a full in-code decomposition of the `of_linearEquiv` finish.

## `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` / `hloc` (line 535)

### Approach (documented in-code)
`of_linearEquiv` route. Goal `hloc(a)`: the pushforward-side restriction
`ρ : Γ(N, ⊤) → Γ(N, D(a))` (R-module via `modulesSpecToSheaf`, `N := (Spec φ)_* M^~`) is a
localization at `powers a`. Build the commuting square with
`e₁ = gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) ⊤`, `e₂ = gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) (D a)`:
`e₂.hom ∘ ρ = (restrictScalars φ σ) ∘ e₁.hom` where `σ` is the `R'`-side restriction
`Γ(M^~,⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(φ a))` (`= (Spec φ)⁻¹ D(a)` by `SpecMap_preimage_basicOpen φ a`). Then
`ρ = e₂.symm ∘ₗ (restrictScalars φ σ) ∘ₗ e₁`; `IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` turns the
`powers (φ a)`-localization `σ` (`tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule`) into a `powers a`-localization
of `restrictScalars φ σ` (using `algebraMapSubmonoid R' (powers a) = powers (φ a)` along
`φ.hom.toAlgebra`), and `of_linearEquiv` / `of_linearEquiv_right` transport it onto `ρ`.

### Result: PARTIAL — the two enabling bricks are built and axiom-clean; `hloc` itself is open.

### Blocker (named, recurring carrier wall)
Two coupled obstacles, both the SAME carrier wall the planner flagged:
1. **The `R`-module structure mismatch.** `restrictScalars φ σ` and the intermediate maps need an
   `R`-action on the `R'`-side sections `Γ(M^~, U)` given by `Module.compHom _ φ.hom` (restriction
   along `φ`). This is **not a synthesizable instance** — supplying it by `letI := Module.compHom _ φ.hom`
   did NOT get picked up by `LinearMap.restrictScalars R` (still "failed to synthesize
   `Module ↑R ↑(Γ(M^~,⊤))`"), because it clashes with the `modulesSpecToSheaf`-supplied `R`-action
   (restriction along the global-sections iso `gsR`) carried on the pushforward side.
2. **The naturality square** `e₂.hom ∘ ρ = (restrictScalars φ σ) ∘ e₁.hom`. The two `R`-actions of (1)
   agree only up to the `eqToIso`/`restrictScalarsComp'App` reconciliation that `gammaPushforwardIsoAt`
   packages; the square therefore cannot be proved without **unfolding `gammaPushforwardIsoAt` and
   invoking naturality of `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App` in its module argument** (a Mathlib
   lemma I did not locate; likely needs to be proved by hand, ~30–50 LOC).

### Next concrete step (for the planner / next prover)
- EITHER prove naturality of `gammaPushforwardIsoAt` in the open (a `NatTrans`-style lemma:
  `gammaPushforwardIsoAt` commutes with the `Γ`-restriction maps on both sides), then the
  `of_linearEquiv` finish above closes `hloc`.
- OR pivot to the **tower** route (avoids the square): show `ρ` peels (by defeq, as the OBJECT does in
  `gammaPushforwardIsoAt`) as `restrictScalars gsR (restrictScalars appTop σ₁)` and apply
  `IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` (R←Γ(SpecR,⊤) via `gsR`) then again
  (Γ(SpecR,⊤)←Γ(SpecR',⊤) via `appTop`) then `IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars` (Γ(SpecR',⊤)←R'
  via `gsR'`, an iso). Requires `letI`s for `Algebra`/`IsScalarTower` at each tower level and the
  submonoid-image equalities (`Submonoid.map_powers`); the risk is the `Module`/`Algebra` instance
  matching that `powers_restrictScalars`/`of_restrictScalars` demand vs. the `restrictScalars`-functor
  module instance — the same wall as (1).

This is the FOURTH recurrence of the smul/Module carrier wall (now: `⊤` → Γ → `D(a)` → `Module R` of a
`φ`-restricted `R'`-module). Per the planner's reversing signal, the architectural path through
`hloc` may itself be the problem; a `gammaPushforwardIsoAt`-naturality lemma is the cleanest
unblock, but if it resists, consider a direct scheme-level `IsQuasicoherent`-of-affine-pushforward
criterion as the route pivot.

## Helpers found / used
- `gammaPushforwardIso` (existing, in-file) — template for `gammaPushforwardIsoAt`.
- `tilde.toOpen`, `tilde.toOpen_res`, the `instance (f:R') : IsLocalizedModule (powers f) (toOpen M (basicOpen f)).hom)` (Mathlib `Tilde.lean:115`).
- `PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one`, `Submonoid.powers_one`, `LinearEquiv.eq_comp_toLinearMap_symm`,
  `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right`, `IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` (in-file),
  `AlgebraicGeometry.SpecMap_preimage_basicOpen` (preimage of basic open).
- `Submonoid.map_powers` — for `algebraMapSubmonoid R' (powers a) = powers (φ a)`.

## Dead ends / warnings
- `LinearMap.comp_apply` / `LinearMap.comp_assoc` / `simp [LinearMap.coe_comp]` do NOT fire on
  `(f ∘ₗ ↑e) x` when `f` is a `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` over a `CommRingCat` coercion — the `∘ₗ` does not
  unify with the `∘ₛₗ` pattern. Use `LinearEquiv.eq_comp_toLinearMap_symm` to rewrite the *equation*
  instead of pointwise (this is how `tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule`'s `key` was closed).
- `simpa only [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one]`/`▸` do NOT rewrite `basicOpen 1 → ⊤` inside the dependent
  `tilde.toOpen M (basicOpen 1)`; use `have h := inferInstance; rw [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one] at h; exact h`.
- `letI : Module R (R'-module) := Module.compHom _ φ.hom` does not get consumed by
  `LinearMap.restrictScalars R` (instance not found) — the `Module.compHom` `R`-action is not
  defeq-recognized against what `restrictScalars` seeks. This is the live carrier wall.

## Informal agent
Attempted (`MOONSHOT_API_KEY` set) but the endpoint returns **HTTP 401 Invalid Authentication** — same
as iter-234/236. No other provider key available.

## Blueprint markers (for the review agent)
- `lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt` is NOT yet a blueprint block — `gammaPushforwardIsoAt` is a new
  project-local declaration realizing blueprint movement (1) `e_{D(a)}`. The plan agent may want to add
  a `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardIsoAt}` block (it generalizes `lem:gammaPushforwardIso`).
- `lem:fromTildeGamma_app_isIso_of_localized` etc. unchanged. `tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule` is
  new; a blueprint block could pin it (it is the `R'`-side input used inside the `hloc` discharge).
- `lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}`) now resolves
  to an existing declaration (previously absent) — but it still contains a `sorry`, so it must NOT get
  `\leanok` (the deterministic `sync_leanok` will handle this correctly).

## Why I stopped
**Partial progress (real code).** Built TWO new axiom-clean declarations
(`gammaPushforwardIsoAt`, `tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule`) — these are blueprint movement (1)
`e_{D(a)}` and the `R'`-side localization input, both genuinely needed and reusable. Created the
blueprint-pinned `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (previously absent) with a full proof skeleton reducing
to `hloc`. Did NOT close `hloc`: it requires either (a) a hand-proved naturality lemma for
`gammaPushforwardIsoAt` in its open argument, or (b) the tower route — both of which run into the
recurring `Module R` / smul carrier wall (4th recurrence). I verified the wall concretely
(`Module.compHom` `letI` not consumed by `LinearMap.restrictScalars`). The two pre-existing sorries
(`affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`, `flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso`) were left untouched as
directed. File compiles cleanly (`lake env lean` exit 0; only `sorry` + deprecation warnings).

**iter-239, `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean` (prove lane).** Built TWO axiom-clean reusable bricks
toward the unconditional `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (`hloc` discharge); `hloc` itself STILL OPEN.
Sorry 2→3 (new sorry is in the new blueprint-pinned `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`).

**Bricks (both axiom-clean, compile):**
- `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ N U : Γ((Spec φ)_* N, U) ≅ restrictScalars φ (Γ(N, (Spec φ)⁻¹ U))` for ANY
  open `U` — the open-indexed generalization of `gammaPushforwardIso` (= blueprint movement (1)
  `e_{D(a)}`). KEY INSIGHT: proof is `gammaPushforwardIso`'s body VERBATIM with `op ⊤ → op U / op V`,
  because `modulesSpecToSheaf` restricts scalars to the base ring UNIFORMLY at the top open (not
  per-`U`), so the `restrictScalarsComp'App`×2 + `eqToIso(globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop)`
  reconciliation is open-independent.
- `tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule M b : IsLocalizedModule (powers b) (Γ(M^~,⊤)→Γ(M^~,D(b)) restriction over R')`.
  Proof: `toOpen M ⊤` is loc at `powers 1` ⇒ bijective; `tilde.toOpen_res` triangle +
  `LinearEquiv.eq_comp_toLinearMap_symm` + `of_linearEquiv_right` transport the known `toOpen(D b)` loc.

**`hloc` finish (documented in-code, NOT closed):** `of_linearEquiv` square
`e₂∘ρ = (restrictScalars φ σ)∘e₁` with `e₁,e₂ = gammaPushforwardIsoAt … ⊤ / (D a)`, `σ` the R'-side
restriction; `powers_restrictScalars` turns σ's `powers(φa)` loc into `powers a` loc of `restrictScalars φ σ`
(`algebraMapSubmonoid R' (powers a)=powers(φa)` via `φ.hom.toAlgebra`), then `of_linearEquiv`×2.

**BLOCKER = 4th recurrence of the carrier wall** (now `⊤`→Γ→`D(a)`→**Module-R-of-φ-restricted-R'-module**):
`LinearMap.restrictScalars R` of an R'-side map needs `Module R (Γ(M^~,U)) := Module.compHom _ φ.hom`,
which is NOT synthesizable AND a `letI` providing it is NOT consumed by `restrictScalars` (clashes with
the `modulesSpecToSheaf` `gsR`-restriction R-action on the pushforward side). The two R-actions agree
only up to `gammaPushforwardIsoAt`'s `eqToIso`/`restrictScalarsComp'App` reconciliation ⇒ the naturality
square can't be proved without unfolding it + a (Mathlib-absent?) naturality lemma for
`ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App`. NEXT: prove `gammaPushforwardIsoAt`-naturality-in-open, OR the
tower route (`powers_restrictScalars`×2 + `of_restrictScalars`, R←Γ(SpecR,⊤)←Γ(SpecR',⊤)←R'), OR pivot
to a direct scheme-level `IsQuasicoherent`-of-affine-pushforward criterion (planner's reversing signal).

**Tactic gotchas:** `LinearMap.comp_apply`/`comp_assoc`/`coe_comp` do NOT fire on `(f ∘ₗ ↑e) x` when `f`
is a `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` over a CommRingCat coercion (∘ₗ won't unify with ∘ₛₗ) — rewrite the EQUATION
via `LinearEquiv.eq_comp_toLinearMap_symm` instead. `simpa[basicOpen_one]`/`▸` won't rewrite inside
dependent `tilde.toOpen M (basicOpen 1)`; use `rw […] at h; exact h`. Informal agent: MOONSHOT key
HTTP 401 (dead, same as iter-234/236). See [[ts236-fbc-gamma-fragment-built]].

Active prover mode: **mathlib-build**

## Your goal

Build project-local Mathlib infrastructure axiom-clean, one step at a time, going **as far as possible** in this iteration. Your output consists of definitions and lemmas that compile without `sorry` and whose `#print axioms` shows only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. You stop only when you hit a genuine mathematical blocker — not because one step is hard.

## Invariant

**No `sorry` in your output.** Every declaration you add must be fully proved. If you cannot close the current step: try alternatives first, then either prove a smaller sub-step or leave the declaration absent. Never add a declaration with `sorry`.

## Workflow

### 1. Orient

- Read `PROGRESS.md` for the objective: what infrastructure is needed, and why.
- Read the relevant blueprint chapter. It often contains the mathematical path to the ingredient you're building.
- Read `task_pending.md` for prior dead ends. Do not repeat them.

### 2. Scout the Mathlib API

Before writing any Lean, search for what already exists:

- `lean_local_search` for related names.
- `lean_leansearch` with a description of the mathematical content.
- `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns.
- Look for near-misses: a lemma that covers a sub-case, an instance you can compose, a `simp` set that contains what you need.

Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.
3. **Verify axiom-cleanliness** immediately after the step compiles: `#print axioms MyLemma`. Any `sorryAx` in the ancestry means the step is not axiom-clean — do not proceed until it is gone.
4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment, TODO, or log entry? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there adjacent declarations in the chain I could attempt with the infrastructure I just built?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before declaring the step impossible?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped? Attempt it — a failed attempt with a named error is better than a comment.

Stop only when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable." **Writing a comment about a possible approach counts as identifying it — which means you should attempt it.**

**You are fully authorized to make every mathematical and structural decision within your assigned file without waiting for the planner.** "I'll let the planner decide which route to take" is never a valid stop reason — pick the most promising route, attempt it, document the outcome. The planner corrects route choices next iter; an idle session corrects nothing.

Stop when you have tried alternatives and cannot make further axiom-clean progress. Before stopping:

- Commit all axiom-clean steps you completed.
- Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with a precise handoff (see Logging below).
- Ensure the file compiles with no `sorry`.

## File structure

Place new declarations under a clearly delimited section:

```lean
/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — <TopicName> -/
```

- Use `private` for helpers with no downstream use outside this file.
- Use `theorem` / `lemma` (non-private) for steps that other files will import.
- Add a one-line docstring to each non-private declaration explaining why it is project-local.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any existing declaration. You may add new declarations freely. You must not rename, re-type, or modify signatures of protected declarations.

## API alignment

Mathlib names and signatures are the most common failure source.

- Verify every name with `lean_local_search` or `lean_loogle` — do not rely on memory.
- Check that typeclass assumptions in your statement match what Mathlib requires.
- For `def`-backed types: Lean may not unfold them for typeclass synthesis; use explicit coercions or `change` / `show` in the proof body.

## Write permissions

Only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror the `.lean` path):

```markdown
# Algebra/MathlibSupplement.lean

## Session summary
- Built axiom-clean: `Foo.bar`, `Foo.baz` (lines 12–40)
- Blocked on: `Foo.qux` — needs `Bar.aux` which requires [precise statement]

## Foo.bar (line 12)
- **Approach:** Direct from `Mathlib.Algebra.X` via `simp [Y]`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean

## Foo.qux (not added)
- **Approach 1:** Tried [route] — FAILED because [reason]
- **Approach 2:** Tried informal agent — suggested [sketch], formalization blocked on [specific gap]
- **Next step:** Build `Bar.aux : [precise type statement]` first
- **Dead end:** Do not retry [route] — [why it fails]
```

One section per declaration attempted. For each: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / NOT ADDED), dead-end warnings, next step.

## End-of-session handoff

**Write a `## Summary` section** before the verdicts: declarations added (count + names), declarations blocked (count + why), sorry count before → after across your file.

**Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest. Only axiom-clean Lean declarations count as progress. Comments, sketches, and log prose do NOT. If you wrote down approaches you didn't attempt, say so:

- `Real progress`: N axiom-clean declarations added — name each one with its line number.
- `Partial progress`: added some declarations, hit a specific blocker on the next — name the blocker precisely (not "it's complex", but the exact type or missing ingredient).
- `Approaches written but not attempted`: identified routes in comments or log but did not attempt them — name each. This is valid but weak; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
- `Blocked — alternatives exhausted`: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), alternative routes tried, all failed — name what was tried, why each failed, and the exact type statement of the next needed ingredient.
- `Infrastructure already exists`: Mathlib has what was needed — cite the exact lemma name and namespace.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction

106	The user provides hints in two places:

108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles

113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.

118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.

130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
